I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:12']
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and 'WiFi' in attrs:
        wifi_val = attrs['WiFi']
        if wifi_val != "u'no'" and wifi_val != 'False' and wifi_val != 'None':
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({"total_wifi": len(wifi_businesses), "wifi_businesses": wifi_businesses[:5]}))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Debug: What WiFi values exist?
wifi_values = []
for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_values.append(wifi_val)

wifi_counts = Counter(wifi_values)

# Debug: What descriptions look like?
sample_descs = []
for biz in all_businesses[:10]:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    sample_descs.append(desc[:200])

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_value_counts': dict(wifi_counts),
    'sample_descriptions': sample_descs,
    'total_businesses': len(all_businesses)
}))

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Debug: What WiFi values exist?
wifi_values = []
for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_values.append(wifi_val)

wifi_counts = Counter(wifi_values)

# Debug: What descriptions look like?
sample_descs = []
for biz in all_businesses[:10]:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    sample_descs.append(desc[:200])

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_value_counts': dict(wifi_counts),
    'sample_descriptions': sample_descs,
    'total_businesses': len(all_businesses)
}))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract WiFi businesses with states from descriptions
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is not "no" or "False"
        if wifi_val is not None and 'no' not in str(wifi_val).lower():
            # Extract state from description using regex
            desc = biz.get('description', '')
            # Look for pattern: "..., ST," or "in ST," or "in City, ST"
            state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
            if state_match:
                state = state_match.group(1)
                wifi_businesses.append({
                    'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                    'name': biz['name'],
                    'state': state,
                    'wifi': wifi_val,
                    'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    rating_str = review.get('rating')
    
    # Find which business this review belongs to
    wifi_biz = next((biz for biz in wifi_businesses if biz['business_ref'] == business_ref), None)
    
    if wifi_biz and rating_str is not None:
        try:
            rating = int(rating_str)
            state = wifi_biz['state']
            
            if state not in state_ratings:
                state_ratings[state] = []
            state_ratings[state].append(rating)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    top_state = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state_name = top_state[0]
    top_state_count = top_state[1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses)
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract WiFi businesses with states from descriptions
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is not "no" or "False"
        if wifi_val is not None and 'no' not in str(wifi_val).lower():
            # Extract state from description using regex
            desc = biz.get('description', '')
            # Look for pattern: "..., ST," or "in ST," or "in City, ST"
            state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
            if state_match:
                state = state_match.group(1)
                wifi_businesses.append({
                    'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                    'name': biz['name'],
                    'state': state,
                    'wifi': wifi_val,
                    'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    rating_str = review.get('rating')
    
    # Find which business this review belongs to
    wifi_biz = next((biz for biz in wifi_businesses if biz['business_ref'] == business_ref), None)
    
    if wifi_biz and rating_str is not None:
        try:
            rating = int(rating_str)
            state = wifi_biz['state']
            
            if state not in state_ratings:
                state_ratings[state] = []
            state_ratings[state].append(rating)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    top_state = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state_name = top_state[0]
    top_state_count = top_state[1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses)
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Debug: Check WiFi values and descriptions
wifi_values = []
state_examples = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_values.append(str(wifi_val))
            desc = biz.get('description', '')
            state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
            if state_match:
                state_examples.append({
                    'name': biz['name'],
                    'wifi': str(wifi_val),
                    'state': state_match.group(1),
                    'desc_snippet': desc[:150]
                })

wifi_counts = Counter(wifi_values)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_value_counts': dict(wifi_counts),
    'state_extraction_examples': state_examples[:10],
    'total_businesses': len(all_businesses)
}))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Debug: Check WiFi values and descriptions
wifi_values = []
state_examples = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_values.append(str(wifi_val))
            desc = biz.get('description', '')
            state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
            if state_match:
                state_examples.append({
                    'name': biz['name'],
                    'wifi': str(wifi_val),
                    'state': state_match.group(1),
                    'desc_snippet': desc[:150]
                })

wifi_counts = Counter(wifi_values)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_value_counts': dict(wifi_counts),
    'state_extraction_examples': state_examples[:10],
    'total_businesses': len(all_businesses)
}))

I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract WiFi businesses with states
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (contains 'free' or 'paid', not 'no')
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state from description
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                # Look for pattern: "in City, ST," - extract the state code
                state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews from user database
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Create mapping of business_ref to state for WiFi businesses
wifi_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_biz_map[biz['business_ref']] = biz['state']

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses by state
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str is not None:
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    top_state = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state_name = top_state[0]
    top_state_count = top_state[1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:5] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

result.preview: {"top_state": "N/A", "wifi_business_count": 0, "average_rating": "N/A", "state_breakdown": {}, "total_wifi_businesses": 0, "all_states_found": [], "sample_wifi": []}

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract WiFi businesses with states - fix state extraction
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state from description using corrected regex
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                # Look for pattern: "in City, ST," - extract the state code
                state_match = re.search(r'in\s+[\w\s\.]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews from user database
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Create mapping of business_ref to state for WiFi businesses
wifi_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_biz_map[biz['business_ref']] = biz['state']

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses by state
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str is not None and rating_str != 'None':
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    sorted_states = sorted(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_state_name = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_count = sorted_states[0][1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

# Debug info
all_states_found = list(state_counts.keys())

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'all_states_found': all_states_found,
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:5] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract WiFi businesses with states - fix state extraction
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state from description using corrected regex
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                # Look for pattern: "in City, ST," - extract the state code
                state_match = re.search(r'in\s+[\w\s\.]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews from user database
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Create mapping of business_ref to state for WiFi businesses
wifi_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_biz_map[biz['business_ref']] = biz['state']

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses by state
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str is not None and rating_str != 'None':
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    sorted_states = sorted(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_state_name = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_count = sorted_states[0][1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

# Debug info
all_states_found = list(state_counts.keys())

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'all_states_found': all_states_found,
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:5] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Debug: Check which businesses have WiFi and what their descriptions look like
wifi_businesses_debug = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                # Try different regex patterns
                state_match1 = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                state_match2 = re.search(r'in\s+[\w\s\.]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                
                wifi_businesses_debug.append({
                    'name': biz['name'],
                    'wifi': wifi_val,
                    'desc': desc,
                    'state_match_simple': state_match1.group(1) if state_match1 else None,
                    'state_match_complex': state_match2.group(1) if state_match2 else None
                })

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_businesses_with_states': wifi_businesses_debug[:10],
    'total_matching': len(wifi_businesses_debug)
}))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Debug: Check which businesses have WiFi and what their descriptions look like
wifi_businesses_debug = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                # Try different regex patterns
                state_match1 = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                state_match2 = re.search(r'in\s+[\w\s\.]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                
                wifi_businesses_debug.append({
                    'name': biz['name'],
                    'wifi': wifi_val,
                    'desc': desc,
                    'state_match_simple': state_match1.group(1) if state_match1 else None,
                    'state_match_complex': state_match2.group(1) if state_match2 else None
                })

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_businesses_with_states': wifi_businesses_debug[:10],
    'total_matching': len(wifi_businesses_debug)
}))

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract WiFi businesses with states - fix regex to find state after "in City," pattern
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state from description - look for pattern: "in City, ST,"
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                # Try regex pattern to capture state after comma
                state_match = re.search(r'\bin\s+[^,]*,\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews from user database
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Create mapping of business_ref to state for WiFi businesses
wifi_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_biz_map[biz['business_ref']] = biz['state']

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses by state
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str is not None and rating_str != 'None':
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    sorted_states = sorted(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_state_name = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_count = sorted_states[0][1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:10] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    sorted_states = sorted(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_state_name = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_count = sorted_states[0][1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:10] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Test state extraction on all businesses first
all_states = []
state_extraction_examples = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    # Try simple pattern: comma followed by 2 uppercase letters followed by comma or end of word
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
        all_states.append(state)
        state_extraction_examples.append({
            'name': biz['name'],
            'state': state,
            'desc_snippet': desc[:100]
        })

state_counts_all = Counter(all_states)

# Now extract WiFi businesses with states
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state from description
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews from user database
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Create mapping of business_ref to state for WiFi businesses
wifi_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_biz_map[biz['business_ref']] = biz['state']

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses by state
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str is not None and rating_str != 'None':
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    sorted_states = sorted(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_state_name = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_count = sorted_states[0][1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'all_states_in_dataset': dict(state_counts_all),
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:10] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Test state extraction on all businesses first
all_states = []
state_extraction_examples = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    # Try simple pattern: comma followed by 2 uppercase letters followed by comma or end of word
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
        all_states.append(state)
        state_extraction_examples.append({
            'name': biz['name'],
            'state': state,
            'desc_snippet': desc[:100]
        })

state_counts_all = Counter(all_states)

# Now extract WiFi businesses with states
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is available (not "no")
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            # Check if it's not "no" - so it should be "free" or "paid"
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state from description
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count WiFi businesses by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews from user database
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Create mapping of business_ref to state for WiFi businesses
wifi_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_biz_map[biz['business_ref']] = biz['state']

# Collect ratings for WiFi businesses by state
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str is not None and rating_str != 'None':
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    sorted_states = sorted(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_state_name = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_count = sorted_states[0][1]
    
    # Calculate average rating for top state
    if top_state_name in state_ratings and state_ratings[top_state_name]:
        avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state_name]) / len(state_ratings[top_state_name])
        avg_rating = round(avg_rating, 2)
    else:
        avg_rating = 'No ratings available'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'all_states_in_dataset': dict(state_counts_all),
    'sample_wifi': wifi_businesses[:10] if wifi_businesses else []
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

if isinstance(businesses_path, str) and businesses_path.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Test the regex pattern on actual data
test_cases = []
wifi_businesses = []

for biz in all_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Try regex pattern to capture state after comma
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
        test_cases.append({
            'name': biz['name'],
            'desc': desc,
            'state_found': state
        })
    
    # Check WiFi
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_val = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi_val is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi_val).lower()
            if 'no' not in wifi_str:
                # Extract state using simple comma pattern
                state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                if state_match:
                    state = state_match.group(1)
                    wifi_businesses.append({
                        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                        'name': biz['name'],
                        'state': state,
                        'wifi': wifi_val,
                        'business_ref': biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                    })

# Count by state
state_counts = Counter([biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses])

# Load reviews
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:14']
reviews = reviews_path if isinstance(reviews_path, list) else []

# Collect ratings by state
wifi_biz_map = {biz['business_ref']: biz['state'] for biz in wifi_businesses}
state_ratings = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref in wifi_biz_map:
        state = wifi_biz_map[business_ref]
        rating_str = review.get('rating')
        if rating_str and rating_str != 'None':
            try:
                rating = int(rating_str)
                if state not in state_ratings:
                    state_ratings[state] = []
                state_ratings[state].append(rating)
            except:
                pass

# Find top state
if state_counts:
    top_state = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state_name = top_state[0]
    top_state_count = top_state[1]
    avg_rating = round(sum(state_ratings.get(top_state_name, [])) / len(state_ratings.get(top_state_name, [])), 2) if state_ratings.get(top_state_name) else 'No ratings'
else:
    top_state_name = 'N/A'
    top_state_count = 0
    avg_rating = 'N/A'

result = {
    'top_state': top_state_name,
    'wifi_business_count': top_state_count,
    'average_rating': avg_rating,
    'state_breakdown': dict(state_counts),
    'test_state_extraction': test_cases[:3]
}

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
